title: #marketing: 3625009876
preview:
Ava Morales: heads-up — webinar signups are getting delayed by enrichment; we keep missing the 1st-touch window.
Ethan Park: ugh. can we do immediate routing + enrichment-as-patch? propose 5m acceptance SLA for the event queue.
Ava Morales: yes. plan: add webhook flag quick_route=true and owner_policy=preserve. HubSpot workflow: if quick_route==true -> round-robin to 'web-event-sdr' and set owner_preserve metadata. route immediately, then apply enrichment patch when ready.
Priya Shah: safe on our side. enrichment job will PATCH only profile fields if owner != null. sample patch payload (escaping creds): {"email":"test@acme.com","profile_complete":"false","lead_score":"42"}. we will NOT touch owner id unless owner==null.
Noah Rivera: fallback: if no accept within 5m -> escalate to 'sdr-overflow' and ping #marketing-sdr. also add slack note: "auto-escalated after 5m no-accept".
lead-bot: webhook smoke ok (200). sample incoming: {"email":"attendee@org.com","quick_route":"true","campaign":"spring-demo-q2"}.
Ethan Park: canary plan: start 3% of registrations with x-rollout=canary header. Monitor: acceptance_rate, median accept_time_ms, owner_change_count.
Priya Shah: also touching SF connector: when HubSpot enrichment upserts into Salesforce, pass preserve_owner=true so SF workflow doesn't reassign. will add mapping rule and a unit test.
Ava Morales: SDR playbook notes: when profile shows profile_complete=false -> 1) verify email/role, 2) call with intro script A, 3) update note "partial profile: follow up". I'll post the short script in #marketing.
Noah Rivera: alert thresholds: median accept_time_ms > 300000 (5m) OR owner_change_rate > 0.05 (5%) in 30m window -> rollback canary and page oncall.
Ethan Park: timeline: implement routing flag & hubspot workflow today, canary tomorrow 09:00, ramp to 25% after 2h if all green.
Ava Morales: great. Priya -- can you confirm SF mapping once done? tagging you. :thumbsup:

title: #marketing: 3490012345
preview:
Ava Chen: Heads up — planning to ship the onboarding welcome drip (3 emails) next Tues. Goal: convert free tier -> active usage. Draft campaign in Figma: https://figma.com/file/xyz. Need final subject lines and APAC send windows.
Diego Morales: Nice. What's the send strategy for APAC? one global send at 09:00 PT or staggered by local time?
Ava Chen: Stagger — want local 9–11am where possible. Ops asked for spike throttling. Proposal: three regional windows (APAC, EMEA, Americas) with 4h padding for overlap.
Priya Singh: For deliverability, prefer smaller batches for APAC ISPs. Can we split APAC into ANZ + SEA + EAS? rough buckets 50k / 75k / 40k.
Samir Patel: Agree. Also add seed list + Gmail inboxing check and a 10k holdback for warmup. I'll add these to the campaign config.
Maya Liu: Subject lines A/B — (A) "Welcome to Redwood — quick setup in 3 steps" (B) "Start generating: your Redwood quickstart" — I lean B, more action-oriented.
Diego Morales: B feels better for activation. Save A for re-engagement sends later.
Priya Singh: Also add a plain-text fallback for heavy-mobile recipients and reduce image payload. Mobile rendering has been a weak signal in previous sends.
Ava Chen: Great. @Samir can you add a send throttle policy: ramp 0->25% in first 30m, 50% in 2h, full after 6h. And set retry on 429 with exponential backoff. Example config snippet: ```send_policy: ramp: [0.25@30m, 0.5@2h, 1@6h] retry: {on: [429], strategy: exponential}```
Samir Patel: On it. Adding a 5% canary across all regions first, then progressive rollout. Will post campaign IDs when ready.
deliverability-bot: deploy scheduled: campaign "onboard-welcome-2026-03" planned for 2026-04-06T14:00:00Z. Seeds passing: gmail, outlook. Spam-trap check: clear. Note: template v1 flagged for high image ratio.
Maya Liu: Can we soften the header copy — currently reads too "enterprise" for our target dev audience. Suggest swap "enterprise-grade" -> "developer-first".
Ava Chen: Ack. I’ll push that copy edit into the doc now. Final ask: QA checklist before launch — https://docs.internal/checklists/onboard-email. Need approvals from @Priya and @Samir by EOD Fri.
Priya Singh: I’ll review tonight and flag any deliverability concerns.
Diego Morales: UTM structure confirmed: source=newsletter, medium=email, campaign=onboard_q2. Redirects via tracker; will export final CSV of recipient buckets.
Ava Chen: Perfect. Locking this thread as the single source of truth for this campaign. :rocket:

title: #marketing: 3310001111
preview:
Maya Patel: Quick check — rehearsal swap proposal. I need to swap demo 2 (interactive routing) from Jasper -> Ava due to a conflict. Can we confirm mic/slide handoff?
Connor Blake: Fine by me, thanks for the heads up. Ava, you good taking the live demo slot at 10:25 PT? :calendar:
Ava Chen: Yep I can take it. Will need the demo tokens and the shortened prompt set by EOD so I can rehearse with the actual model.
Maya Patel: Sending the prompt pack now. Also note: red-team plan — we want a fallback model and an automated failover script in case the hosted endpoint spikes.
Jasper Reed: For fallback use: redwood-dedicated-small (quantized). It's compatible with the demo prompts and faster cold-start. I'll drop the curl and a tiny healthcheck script below.
Jasper Reed: ```bash
# healthcheck (polls live endpoint)
STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://api.redwood.ai/v1/health)
if [ "$STATUS" -ne 200 ]; then
  # switch to dedicated fallback
  echo "Failing over to redwood-dedicated-small"
  # call routing api to swap
fi
```
Li Wang: OpsBot, please create a quick alert in #temp-alerts when latency > 600ms for the demo route. Tag: @marketing
OpsBot: Alert rule created: channel=#temp-alerts threshold=600ms route=webinar-demo-2026-04-02
Connor Blake: Logistics: Ava will share screen, Jasper will monitor healthchecks and be ready to trigger the failover, Maya will moderate Qs. We should run one full run at 09:30 PT on the day.

Maya Patel: Budget approved for rehearsal tokens. Connor I'll send you the billing scope. Also, recording note: please record both system audio and presenter mic separately for edit.
Jasper Reed: I'll be on standby for the 09:30 dry run and the main show. Quick test link for everyone: https://staging.redwood.ai/webinar-demo (use acct: webinar-test)
Connor Blake: Perfect. If no objections I'll lock the run-of-show with the swap and post the final cue sheet by 6pm PT. :thumbsup:
Li Wang: +1
Ava Chen: Thanks — I'll start rehearsing with the prompt pack now.

preview:
Ava Chen: Heads up — the weekly digest shows a weird spike in inbound MQLs from the EU cohort this morning; chart: https://pulseboard.redwood/series/launch-ops?range=24h
Ben Ortiz: saw that. Source filter looks like 'webinar-signup' but CVR is low. Could be duped UTM params or fb redirect issue :thinking_face:

Lina Gomez: tagging in case analytics can confirm: @mkt-analytics any chance a campaign tag change rolled out overnight?
mkt-bot: deploy-notice — /deploy prod/marketing/utm-normalizer v3.1 promoted by devops at 03:02 UTC
Ben Ortiz: that deploy could be it. v3 had a new fallback for missing utm_source.

Ava Chen: ugh — so these are inflating paid-webinar numbers. we need to correct attribution and update the pulseboard widget to exclude unknown-web or map it to organic.

Samir Patel: starting backfill now, will ping when done — ETA 45m.

Samir Patel: backfill confirmed. re-run numbers: organic MQLs -1100; paid-webinar down 980. adjusted revenue projection changes in digest: -$1.8k today. will attach the CSV.
Lina Gomez: perfect. i'll update the weekly KPI thread and add a short note to the exec snapshot. moving forward let's include a UTM sanity check in pre-launch checklist. :white_check_mark:
Ben Ortiz: sweet. closing this thread once digest is updated.
Ava Chen: thanks everyone :pray:

title: #marketing: 2999999999
preview:
Maya Patel: quick idea — run a narrow PLG microtest pairing a 25m "Starter Sprint" workshop + 6 starter prompts + small credits to nudge first-gen and trial starts.
Jon Rivera: sample & arms?
Maya Patel: ~4.2k recent signups (0–12d). three arms: control, email + 6 credits + replay link, in-app DM + 6 credits + one-click import of prompt pack + live workshop invite.
Ava Chen: Workshop: 25m total — 10m demo, 10m live build, 5m Q&A. Will feature a support summarizer and a code->explain prompt.
Ravi Singh: routing: use quickform RSVP with calendar embed. want to test delivery channel effect (email vs in-app). Need import endpoint to track prompt_imported.
Priya Nair: benchmarks/launch post will be small: 2 charts + TL;DR showing sample time-to-first-gen improvements with starter prompts. Post links to replay+pack.
growth-bot: experiment created: id=exp_startersprint_2999999999, variants=3, start=2999999999, status=scheduled

```
variants: [control, email_credits_replay, inapp_credits_import]
audience_filter: signup_days <= 12
metrics: trial_start_7d, prompt_imported, prompt_runs_2, time_to_first_gen
```
Jon Rivera: success criteria: >=12% uplift in trial_start_7d OR >=20% lift in prompt_imported vs control.
Ava Chen: prompt pack path: /share/promptpacks/startersprint-v1. will add README and one-click import metadata.

title: #marketing: 2987650000
preview:
Ava Chen: Quick ask — we need a multiformat exec asset pack for the benchmark launch next week. Includes: email teaser, hero for social, webinar slide diagram (latency vs cost), a one-pager PDF, and 3 social motion GIFs. :sparkles:

Ava Chen: Requirements: localized copy (EN/FR/DE/JP), brand compliance check, export-ready files (SVGs for diagrams + PNG/JPEG web sizes), light/dark colorways, and accessibility alt text. Can design own mock copy if content isn't final but flag it.

Diego Rivera: Got it. Can you drop a Figma file or starter frames? Any hard constraints on illustration style or existing icon set to use?

Ava Chen: Figma starter here: https://www.figma.com/file/benchmark-xyz/benchmark-assets — I added a rough hero and a sketch of the latency-cost curve. Use the brand icon set in the "components/icons" page.

Priya Nair: I'll paste the copy for the hero + email subject lines below. Use these as primary: 
- Hero headline: "Inference that balances speed and unit cost"
- Subhead: "Ship features faster with predictable latency and lower per-token spend"
- Email subject (A): "See how we cut inference costs without sacrificing latency"
- Email subject (B): "Benchmark: throughput, latency, and the economics of inference"

Diego Rivera: Perfect. For the webinar diagram — do we want an annotated curve (points for host mode vs quantized) or a clean chart? Also: SVG text or outlines?

Ava Chen: Annotated curve preferred with 3 callouts (Hosted API, Dedicated, Private). Keep text live in SVG (not outlines) so we can localize later. Callouts should have short copy — Priya can provide.

Priya Nair: Callouts: 
- Hosted: "fastest time-to-value"
- Dedicated: "predictable throughput, isolation"
- Private: "compliance & control"

title: #marketing: 2934567890
preview:
Clara Ito: Quick sync — planning the benchmark launch nurture. Need newsletter + webinar sequence that works across NA/EU/APAC. Key goals: maximize webinar RSVPs, local attendance, and report downloads. Thinking: language-aware segmentation, 3 pre-event emails + 2 post-event followups, plus calendar ICS and timezone-aware reminders.
Mateo Alvarez: I can own segmentation & send windows. Proposed segments: en-US, en-EU (UK/IE), es-ES/LA, ja, and de. Also a separate "customer-high-touch" segment routed via AEs with private RSVP links.
Priya Shah: Copy plan: create compact variants per language and then a brevity experiment — short subject + snappy body vs longer persuasive copy. A/B test copy length within each language on engaged cohorts. For subjects: include local timezone mention when appropriate, e.g., "Live: Benchmark walkthrough — 10am BST / 2am PT".
Greg Novak: ESP notes: dynamic content blocks supported for 5 locales. For any locale not available default to English. Calendar add: include ics and Google Calendar links; watch URL length for some providers. Also ensure encoded UTF-8 subject support for non-Latin scripts.
Deliverability Bot: Deliverability checks: seed tests in localized inboxes (Gmail US, Gmail JP, GMX DE), check feedback loop rates, and monitor for character-set drop issues. Avoid long quoted-printable encoded subjects (>75 chars) which some ISPs truncate. For large sends, throttle per-region to avoid ISP soft-bans.
Clara Ito: Segmentation filters (export-ready) — keep simple:
```
segment_en_us: locale:en-US AND opt_in:newsletter AND engagement_60d:gte1
segment_ja: locale:ja AND opt_in:newsletter AND engagement_90d:gte1
segment_customer_high: tag:customer AND ae_owner:exists
```
Mateo Alvarez: Send windows (local): NA customers -> Tue 10:00 PT, EU locales -> Wed 11:00 BST, APAC locales -> Thu 09:00 JST. For broad newsletter cohorts send at local morning and schedule throttles: max_chunk=15k, pause=10m.
Priya Shah: Testing plan: within each language run a brevity A/B (Short vs Long) seeded with 10% sample, pick winner at 18h. For subjects also test "calendar-in-subject" vs no-calendar tag on high-touch customers. Track opens -> RSVP -> attendance funnel.
Greg Novak: QA checklist: localized seeds (support@, product@, 1 regional inbox per locale), verify charset in subject for JP/DE, test ICS & Google calendar links, verify personalization tokens in each locale fallback. Also validate List-Unsubscribe header and plain-text fallbacks.
Deliverability Bot: Suppress rules: downloaded_report_45d:true, unsub_product:true, hard_bounce_90d:true, spam_trap_flag:true. For locales with strict consent (DE) double-check legal opt-in fields.
Clara Ito: Ops Bot — create campaign skeleton with locale sub-campaigns and tasks: segments (Mateo), copy briefs & brevity test (Priya), deliverability QA (Greg). Set dry-run 6 days before primary send.
Ops Bot: Campaign draft created: "Global Benchmark Launch — Multi-locale Newsletter + Webinar Nurture". Tasks added: segmentation (Mateo), copy & tests (Priya), deliverability QA (Greg). Dry-run scheduled 6 days before send.
Mateo Alvarez: Note on metrics: report downloads tracked via utm_campaign=bench_global_launch, utm_medium=email, utm_source=newsletter, utm_content={{locale}}_{{variant}}. For AE links include ae_owner param.
Priya Shah: I'll drop short-long copy variants for en/ja/de/es in the doc today. Keep images lightweight for locales with higher mobile rates.
Greg Novak: Final ops note: run localized seed spam check 48h prior. If any locale seed spam rate >0.35% hold that locale. Also schedule staggered sends by region to smooth SMTP load.
Clara Ito: Perfect — small, focused sends per locale, brevity test, calendar links, and AE private invites. Thanks everyone, dropping initial copy in doc by EOD. :earth_americas:

title: #marketing: 2612345678
preview:
Maya Rivera: Quick idea — want to run a 'warm pulse' weekend send for trial signups this Friday. Short handshake email + 3-day micro-nurture. Goal: convert high-intent -> webinar RSVP, others -> standard nurture. Thoughts on staggering windows to avoid queue spikes? :wave:

Sam Chen: Love it. My rec is 3 cohorts by activity signals: (A) product tour / API used (high intent), (B) signed up but no usage (warm), (C) partner/marketing signups (cold). Stagger sends by cohort to smooth throughput and give each cohort time to react. :+1:

Sam Chen: Proposed windows (rough):
```
Cohort A: Fri 11:00-11:30 PST
Cohort B: Fri 12:00-13:00 PST
Cohort C: Sat 09:00-10:30 PST
```

Priya Natarajan: For subject lines / CTA split: test urgency vs. value. Two quick variants: 1) "Welcome — quick setup guide + live office hours" 2) "Get the most from your trial in 5 minutes". A/B with RSVP CTA vs. product-setup CTA. Keep body <= 120 words.

Elena Gomez: We'll need tracking tags: utm_campaign=warm-pulse-2612, cohort= A|B|C, email_variant=A|B. Add an event for "handshake_rsvp" and "micro_nurture_open" so growth can measure lift. Also plan a fallback path if SES bounce rate spikes.

Ash (Growth): Suggest we add a tiny randomized delay per message (0-45m) within the scheduled window to avoid batch bursts — basically a Poisson-ish spread. Low overhead, big smoothing benefit. Also think about prioritizing webhook/eng routes for Cohort A so they get lower latency landing experiences.

Maya Rivera: Draft handshake copy (short):
"Hey {{first_name}} — welcome! We noticed you started a trial. Join a 20-min walkthrough this week to get live tips and a free prompt pack. RSVP -> [link]. If you prefer, try our 2-minute quickstart guide"

Sam Chen: Love that. For the micro-nurture cadence: Day 0 handshake, Day 1 quick tip, Day 3 use-case story + invite, Day 7 re-engage with case study. Keep each email focused on one CTA.

Priya Natarajan: One more ask — can marketing ops actually create the segments today so we can QA on Wed? If yes, I'll prepare the 2 subject variants and schedule the test cell (~10% each) and control for measurement.

Elena Gomez: Ops can create segments today. I'll add the tracking and set alarm for delivery failure >1% and bounce >2%.

Ash (Growth): I'll own the KPI doc: open, click, RSVP rate, conversion to active usage at D7. Will drop a short dashboard link here when ready.

Maya Rivera: Perfect. Action items:
- Ops: create segments + tracking (Elena)
- Copy: Priya to deliver two subject variants + 3 micro-emails
- Send cadence + throttling: Sam to finalize windows and jitter setup
- KPIs: Ash to create dashboard

Maya Rivera: If we can QA by Thu EOD and approve, schedule for Fri morning batch for Cohort A. Will we route webinar invites to a special landing page? (prefer yes)

Elena Gomez: Yes — route to webinar landing with prefilled params. Will confirm page capacity.

Sam Chen: OK — I'll post final windows + the jitter script in #eng-ops once QA passes. :rocket:

Maya Rivera: Thanks all — excited to see if a tight weekend pulse moves trial conversion. :tada:

title: #marketing: 2024501122
preview:
Olivia Park: Quick sync on launch newsletter + 7-day drip for webinar no-shows. Goal: convert product watchers into trial users. Need segment rules, cadence, and warmup plan.
Sanjeev Rao: Agree. We should split no-shows by engagement: watched >30s vs landed only. Different subject/tone.
Tess Morgan: Copy idea for watched >30s: 'You missed the live demo — clips & benchmarks inside' For landed-only: 'See what you missed: 3-minute benchmark highlights'
Priya Natarajan: Segment sizes: watched>30s = 1.8k, landed-only = 3.4k, registrants who converted = 600 (suppress). Also compile a control holdback of 5% per segment for lift measurement.
Micah Lee: Drip cadence proposal: Day0 (send recap & clips), Day2 (short benchmark email + CTA to trial), Day5 (customer story + limited time credit). For no-shows, use lower frequency.
Olivia Park: Deliverability: we need to warm up by sending small batches to high-engagement users first. Plan: send 5% to top-engaged, wait 15m, then 20%, then remainder over 2 hours. Ops rate limit 900/min.
Sanjeev Rao: Also set seed list to major ISPs and internal inboxes to check rendering and spam traps. Alert if bounce rate >2% in first 30m.
Tess Morgan: A/B test subject + CTA placement. Test subject (A): 'Benchmark: 2x latency, 3x cost savings'. (B): 'Redwood 2026: faster inference for production' CTA test: hero CTA vs inline CTA.
Priya Natarajan: UTM params: utm_campaign=launch_march2026_drip, utm_medium=email, utm_source=newsletter. Add campaign tags to CRM so ops can attribute conversions.
Olivia Park: Suppress list: customers with active contract, unsubscribed, bounced last 90d, and people already in paid-trial funnel.
BrandBot: New campaign draft created: https://app.redwood/marketing/campaigns/2024501122 (id 8941) :white_check_mark:
Micah Lee: Sample campaign config for ops (paste into campaign manager):
```
name: "launch_march2026_drip"
segments:
  - webinar_no_show_watched
  - webinar_no_show_landed
exclude:
  - registrants_converted
  - active_customers
holdback: "5%"
send_strategy:
  - stage: "seeding"
    percents: [5,20,75]
    pause_minutes: 15
  - stage: "remainder"
    window_minutes: 120
ab_tests:
  - subject: ["A","B"]
  - cta_position: ["hero","inline"]
utm: "utm_campaign=launch_march2026_drip&utm_source=newsletter"
ops_alerts:
  - bounce_rate_threshold: "2%"
  - deliverability_drop: "15%"
```
Olivia Park: Nice. Last call for copy by 14:00 PT. I'll approve final send plan at 16:00 PT if no red flags. :eyes:

title: #marketing: 1943216789
preview:
Ava Chen: Quick sync — want to run a small PLG experiment next week that combines a 15-min microwebinar + staggered free credits to boost onboarding completion. Thoughts?
Samir Patel: love the idea. what's the hypothesis? more attendees convert vs just credits?
Ava Chen: hypothesis: webinar drives intent signal so we can surface higher-value prompt templates in onboarding -> higher DAU in week 1. credits act as friction remover.
Maya Lopez: can we keep the cohort small (500) and split into 3 arms: webinar+credits, credits-only, control? run for 2 weeks.
Ethan Brooks: sounds good. need routing plan for inbound leads — which signal triggers webinar invite vs immediate credits?
Samir Patel: propose rule: if sign-up via webinar ad or utm=webinar -> enroll in webinar arm; organic signups -> credits-only unless they click webinar CTA.
Maya Lopez: also add traffic control: cap webinar invites to 300 per week; overflow goes to waitlist + immediate small credit ($5)
Ava Chen: can product support templated prompt pack push during onboarding? ideally show 3 curated templates after account creation for webinar cohort.
Ethan Brooks: yes — onboarding flow has a feature flag. I'll add a flag `exp/webinar_prompt_pack_v1` and a rollout plan.
Samir Patel: draft experiment config below — please react with :thumbsup: if we can run it.
```yaml
experiment_id: onboarding_webinar_credit_stack_1943
arams:
  cohort_size: 500
  arms:
    - name: webinar_plus_credits
      promo_credit_usd: 20
      webinar_invite: true
    - name: credits_only
      promo_credit_usd: 20
      webinar_invite: false
    - name: control
      promo_credit_usd: 0
      webinar_invite: false
metrics:
  - week1_active_users
  - pct_prompt_template_use
  - avg_spend_30d
traffic_rules:
  - rule: utm_source==webinar -> assign webinar_plus_credits
  - rule: organic_with_webinar_cta -> assign webinar_plus_credits
  - else -> random_assign(credits_only, control)
```
Maya Lopez: small ask — include a soft-enrollment email sequence for webinar arm: reminder at -24h, -1h, and replay link.
Ava Chen: yep add replay tracking as a metric.
Ethan Brooks: deploy-bot: can you create the experiment flag and a canary rollout for 50 users first?

Samir Patel: on it. will add event names: webinar_invited, webinar_attended, prompt_template_used, credit_redeemed.

intent = form['lead_intent']

```
- Confirm RTMP/Zoom ingest + recording path
- Verify slideshare permissions for host and producer
- Run quick infer runtime smoke test (p95 < 350ms)
- Confirm fallback clips accessible by stage mgr
- Test inbound form webhook and SDR routing (dry run)
```

Ava Chen: let's use p95 in the one-pager and keep p99 in the appendix for big customers. Example copy: "Typical p95 token latency: Hosted ~230ms, Dedicated ~180ms, Private (on-prem tuned) ~210ms."

Sam Patel: Also include a sentence about graceful degradation: "Automatic regional fallback and model-variant routing preserve availability under regional capacity constraints." Helps with uptime questions.

else:
  route_to = "growth-sales"
```

preview:
Rae Kim: quick sync — we need a tight benchmark narrative for the launch post + one hero microchart for slides. goal: make it obvious why serving choices matter to app owners. who wants to own copy vs data vs design?
Diego Santos: happy to own the data slice. recommend highlighting: median token latency, 95th tail, and normalized $/1k tokens. keep the chart uncluttered 1: single visual that pairs 95th latency (ms) with cost per 1k on a secondary axis.
Aisha Qureshi: I'll take copy. short headline + three bullets (what we measured, why it matters, how to learn more). also want a short note about "graceful fallback" behavior for reliability-conscious buyers.
Ben Carter: design here. thinking a split hero: left=big number pair (95th: 120ms, $/1k: $0.75), right=mini combo-line chart showing hosted/dedicated/private lines. variants for social, blog, slide.
Rae Kim: let's avoid exact numbers in socials unless vetted. diego, can you run a quick microbench and post the CSV? we need numbers for the slide and a one-sentence explainer per datapoint.
Diego Santos: will run microbench today. baseline config: seq=256, batch auto, KV cache warm. sample output I'll share as CSV. quick mock now: "model,mode,median_ms,95pct_ms,cost_per_1k" then rows.
Aisha Qureshi: copy draft suggestion: headline: "Serve smarter: cut latency and token cost where it counts" bullets: "We benchmarked token-level latency and per-1k token cost across hosted, dedicated, and private deployments." "Small infra changes (batching, quant) can move the needle on both user latency and monthly token bills." "Join our webinar for methodology + live Q&A." CTA: "Register: https://redwood.example/webinar-bench"
Ben Carter: mock sizes: social card 1200x628, hero 1600x600, deck 1280x720. Will deliver mock PNGs and source fig.
Rae Kim: routing note 1 where do registrations land? want a hidden field for "infra type interest" to route inbound. and tag "bench-launch".
Aisha Qureshi: inbound plan: HubSpot form -> tag=bench-launch -> immediate confirm email -> 48hr reminder -> 1hr before reminder. if email domain matches enterprise list, add to SDR queue. also capture pain point (latency/cost/reliability/security) one-click.
Diego Santos: sample CSV I'll post in channel after run. quick synthetic example for design to mock: ```model,mode,median_ms,95pct_ms,cost_per_1k
mistral-8b,hosted,22,85,0.95
mistral-8b,dedicated,18,70,0.65
mistral-8b,private,20,80,0.75
```
Ben Carter: perfect. Rae: should we use "up to" phrasing in social? keeps us safe.
Rae Kim: yes. copy rule: use "up to" or ranges and link to methodology on landing page. landing page must include seq length, batch config, quant settings.
Aisha Qureshi: landing page microcopy: short explainer of methodology + link to repo. include a small footnote: "Results vary by model, workload, and sequence length."
webhook-bot: Webinar scheduled: "Inference Benchmarks: Latency & Cost Playbook" on 2026-04-14 11:00 PDT. Reg page: https://redwood.example/webinar-bench
Diego Santos: timeline: I'll have CSV + short blurb by EOD. Ben can start mock visuals with placeholder numbers. Aisha will finalize copy once Diego confirms numbers.
Ben Carter: will prepare three mock variants and label layers for quick swaps. will add tooltip text for each datapoint: "hosted/dedicated/private; seq=256; batching=auto"
Rae Kim: final ask: what's the single quick takeaway on the hero? I'm leaning to "95th latency vs $/1k" pair as the headline metric. thoughts?
Aisha Qureshi: Agreed. that'll speak to both engineers (latency) and finance/biz (cost).
Diego Santos: agreed. I'll make sure cost is normalized per 1k tokens and latency shown as ms.
Rae Kim: thanks everyone 1 reconvene after Diego posts results. keep copy conservative and methodology transparent. :white_check_mark:

title: #marketing: 1769804321
preview:
Maya Patel: Quick sync — proposing a combined webinar + benchmark narrative to fuel PLG funnel experiments. Top-level goal: webinar signups -> deliver starter prompt templates + 7-day free credits -> first API call within 48h. Hypothesis: starter prompt pack in the post-webinar flow increases activation rate by ~15% compared to credits-only.

Ravi Singh: Love the framing. What baseline numbers are we comparing to?

Ava Chen: Baseline from last quarter: webinar -> signup 18%, signup -> activation (1st API call) 4%. Cost per activated user ~ $420. Need to bring activation up or CAC will spike.

Deon Brooks: Thinking of a 3-arm experiment: A) credits + email w/ prompt pack, B) credits + in-console guided tour + contextual nudge, C) credits + 30min office hours slot + prompt pack. Run equal allocation, measure 7d activation and 30d usage.

Maya Patel: Timeline: run a single webinar (benchmarks + 2 live demos) week of Apr 6. Landing page + thank-you page must capture intent signals (company, estimated monthly requests, use-case). Post-webinar seq: day0 immediate email w/ prompt pack, day1 in-app nudge, day3 tips + invite.

Ravi Singh: Routing rules for inbound leads (sketch):
- if company_size >= 100 and est_requests_per_month >= 500 -> route to sales-dedicated queue
- else if company_email present and uses promo_code -> route to growth-nurture
- else -> automated nurture funnel (email + in-app)
Will create a lightweight webhook mapping for the form.

Ava Chen: We should pass the experiment arm in the lead meta so support/growth knows the variant. Also include utm_campaign and webinar_id.

Deon Brooks: Quick pseudo-rule to attach arm:
```
if webinar_rsvp and random_number < 0.333 -> arm A
elif webinar_rsvp and random_number < 0.666 -> arm B
else -> arm C
```
Add arm tag to user metadata for analytics.

onboard-bot: Reminder: Webinar "Benchmarks that Move the Needle" scheduled Thu Apr 9 10:00am PT — calendar invites sent to presenters. :bell:

Maya Patel: Messaging thread for launch post and benchmark narrative: need a short headline (tweet-style), 1-paragraph TL;DR for landing, and 3 takeaway bullets for slides. Draft headline: "How teams cut text-gen cost 3x while improving latency — live benchmarks + playbook." Thoughts?

Ravi Singh: Snappy. Maybe soften "cut cost 3x" -> "reduce per-token cost up to 3x" to avoid overclaim. Add CTA: "Reserve your seat + get a starter prompt pack"

Ava Chen: For the email copy we'll A/B subject lines: S1 = "Starter prompts + free credits — join Apr 9 webinar" S2 = "See our live benchmarks — get prompt templates & credits". We'll track open -> click -> activation funnel.

Deon Brooks: Analytics: capture cohort by arm, webinar attendance, prompt-download, credit-redemption, first API call timestamp, and 7d token usage. We'll instrument in Growth Analytics dashboard.

Maya Patel: Owners: Landing + registration form = @Ravi (draft by Fri), Post-webinar email sequence + prompt pack = @Ava (draft by Mon), Routing/webhook + arm tagging = @Deon (integration by Tue), Slides + benchmark narrative = @Maya (draft + data by Wed).

Ravi Singh: I'll spin up the landing with hidden fields for est_requests and utm params. Sharing the form link here when done.

Ava Chen: I'll draft two email variants and the prompt pack (3 starter prompts + usage notes).

Maya Patel: Decision checklist before launch: 1) form + routing tested, 2) arm assignment logic in analytics, 3) email variants uploaded, 4) in-app nudge snippet ready. We should run a soft QA with 20 internal test signups.

onboard-bot: Added task: #marketing-webinar-17698 -> "soft QA signups" assigned to Maya, deadline Tue Apr 7.

Deon Brooks: Small ops note — make sure credit issuance is idempotent (we had a double-issue bug last time). I'll add a quick guard in the webhook: issue_credit(user_id) only if user.metadata.credit_issued != true.

Maya Patel: Final ask — who can own tracking the experiment? Need daily standup updates for first week after webinar. @Deon?

Deon Brooks: I can own tracking + dashboard. Will post daily digest in #marketing for 7 days.

Maya Patel: Perfect. If no blockers, we'll lock assets by EOD Fri and run the webinar Apr 9. :rocket:

Ravi Singh: Sounds good. I'll post the landing link once up.

Ava Chen: On it — will share draft email + prompt pack for review tomorrow. :memo:

Maya Patel: Thanks team. Quick react with :thumbsup: if you agree to deadlines.

title: #marketing: 1768930200
preview:
Ben Carter: putting together the FINAL sprint readout (Week 4) today. need inputs by 3pm PT so Camila can review before we share tomorrow.

Ben Carter: doc will follow the standard template + 1-slide exec summary. pls drop:
1) what shipped (links)
2) results (best estimate + caveats)
3) decision (keep/iterate/kill)
4) follow-ons

Ben Carter: draft readout doc (WIP): https://drive.google.com/file/d/1lifecycle-growth-final-readout-wip
Ben Carter: funnel dashboard: https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/activation-funnel-q1

Tyler Benson: for paid/attribution section, here’s the Week 3–4 rollup (post-UTM cleanup):
- Spend cap held: ~$18.5k total (under $20k)
- Best audience: signup-no-activation (1–7d lookback)
- CPA to “first successful request”: ~12–15% lower vs broad site visitors (varies by platform)
- Creative: “Ship your first request in 5 minutes” headline consistently beat the “latency/cost” one on CTR

Tyler Benson: caveat: attribution is directional; we’re relying on UTMs + last-touch in HubSpot. will add in readout as such.

Sergio Costa: dashboard note: numbers look stable after the dedupe fix (PR-28521). I pulled a snapshot for the readout timeframe (Jan 14 → Feb 7) and saved panels here: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1activation-dashboard-screenshots

Sergio Costa: biggest shifts:
- signup → api_key_created improved slightly after console nudge rollout
- api_key_created → first_success improved most from email v2 cohort

Paula Mendes: +1, but please include the footnote: pre-fix (before Jan 24) signup and first_success were inflated in one region due to duplicate event emission. after fix we re-baselined. if anyone asks “why did activation drop overnight”, that’s why.

Paula Mendes: proposed metric phrasing for readout:
- Primary: % of new signups reaching first_success within 7d
- Secondary: % reaching 100k_tokens within 7d, TTFT (p50), D7 active usage

Paula Mendes: also: we should call out that D30 is not fully mature for later cohorts yet, so it’s trend-only.

Marissa Cole: console nudges section draft:
Shipped:
- new nudge component + targeting/holdout (behind `lifecycle_growth_nudges_v1`)
- 4 nudge variants tested: create API key, run first request, enable streaming, view usage dashboard
Results:
- CTR strongest: API key + First request (streaming was meh; usage dashboard got clicks but low downstream)
Decision:
- propose graduating API key + First request nudges to always-on onboarding for self-serve Hosted API
- keep streaming + usage dashboard behind flag for iteration (copy + placement)

Marissa Cole: link to spec: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/PROD/Console+Onboarding+Nudges+Framework

Dylan Brooks: eng addendum: dismissal persistence bug is fixed (the “appears on wrong page” edge case). flag targeting is now by cohort assignment + region guard.

Dylan Brooks: for readout, suggest we explicitly state holdout: 10% holdout for nudges, 5% holdout for email (so we can keep learning without stopping everything).

Soojin Lee: messaging notes for final narrative:
- lead with “get to first working request fast” (time-to-value)
- then “predictable latency + cost” as the reasons to stick around
- avoid over-claiming “fastest” or hard promises in lifecycle touches; use “optimized for production” / “Redwood-verified profiles”

Soojin Lee: also recommend standardizing “first successful request” wording across email + console + landing pages (some variants said “first API call”). small thing but it shows up in support questions.

Tessa Morgan: creative / assets status:
- email template refresh shipped (mobile spacing + CTA button styles). DES-311 closed.
- retargeting kit shipped (smaller “developer-first” variant set requested in thread 1768680044). exports are in the same Drive folder.

Tessa Morgan: if we want a follow-on experiment: add a single graphic/diagram in Email 2 (API key → hello world → usage dashboard) — could reduce confusion for folks who haven’t done this before.

Jordan Blake: reminder for readout: call out the retargeting guardrails + what we DIDN’T do.
- excluded active opps + enterprise domains (weekly refresh)
- excluded regulated segments where tracking consent is unclear
- no targeting of known POCs

Jordan Blake: sales feedback: fewer “why am I seeing ads while in procurement” pings than last quarter. please keep that sentence in there so we don’t regress.

Camila Reyes: for the exec summary, I want 3 bullets:
1) what moved the primary KPI (and by how much, even if it’s a range)
2) what we’re making always-on vs stopping
3) the top 3 follow-ons + resourcing ask (if any)

Camila Reyes: and make the caveats explicit (event fix, D30 maturity). we should be proud but precise.

Ben Carter: super helpful, thx all.

Ben Carter: here’s the structure I’m putting in the readout (so you know what I’ll quote):
- What shipped (email v2 + console nudges + retargeting + instrumentation/dashboard)
- Results summary (activation + TTFT + early retention signals)
- Guardrails status (deliverability / opt-out / audience exclusions)
- Decisions
- Follow-ons

Ben Carter: preliminary “decision” draft (pls react if you disagree):
KEEP:
- Email v2 winner variant for new signups (and expand to stalled-users segment next)
- Always-on console nudges: API key + First request
ITERATE:
- Streaming nudge (placement + better proof/benefit)
- Retargeting: focus budget on signup-no-activation + tighten creative to “first request”
KILL/PAUSE:
- broad site visitor retargeting (too noisy + worse CPA)

Ben Carter: follow-ons I’m listing (top candidates):
1) stalled-user rescue path (no api_key in 24h / no first_request in 72h) — email + console
2) onboarding checklist v1 (2–3 steps max) in console, tied to same events
3) landing page/quickstart alignment (make the “hello world” snippet the canonical CTA everywhere)

Ben Carter: if anyone has strong opinions on order/owners, reply here. otherwise I’ll finalize and send to Camila for review at ~3:30pm PT.

Tyler Benson: +1 on pausing broad visitor retargeting. I’ll add a short note on why (attribution noise + higher support risk).

Paula Mendes: decisions look right. I’ll drop the exact metric definitions + the “post-dedupe” rebaseline note into the doc.

Marissa Cole: ordering works. I can own the checklist follow-on (product) if we keep it lightweight + behind a flag.

Camila Reyes: approved direction. just make sure the exec slide has numbers in ranges and the caveat line. :thumbsup:

Ben Carter: awesome. locking by EOD. thanks everyone.

title: #marketing: 1768672201
preview:
Tyler Benson: Need to lock UTM conventions + landing page mapping for the retargeting launch. Last chance before I bake into HubSpot + ad platform templates.

Ben Carter: yep let’s do it. biggest thing: keep campaigns readable in dashboard + avoid 17 variants. what’s your proposed schema?

Tyler Benson: Proposal (consistent across LinkedIn/Google/Reddit):
- `utm_source`: `linkedin` | `google` | `reddit`
- `utm_medium`: `paid_social` (LI/Reddit) | `paid_search` (Google)
- `utm_campaign`: `ri_retgt_q1_2026_activation` (one umbrella)
- `utm_content`: `{audience}-{creative}-{cta}` (no spaces, dashes ok)
- `utm_term`: only for Google (keyword)
Also add `utm_id` optional later; not required for v1.

Soojin Lee: +1 on readable. Can we standardize audience names so they match our internal segments? i.e., `signup_no_activation` vs `signup-no-activation` etc.

Tyler Benson: Yep. Canonical audience slugs:
1) `site_visitor` (docs + marketing site)
2) `signup_no_activation` (signed up, no first successful request)
3) `activated_no_return` (first success, no activity in 7d)
Creative slugs: `perf` (latency), `cost`, `reliability`, `devex`.
CTA slugs: `quickstart`, `console`, `pricing`.
Example: `utm_content=signup_no_activation-devex-quickstart`

Camila Reyes: Works. Keep `utm_campaign` stable so we can roll up spend/CPA. If we need sub-campaigns later we can do it with `utm_content`.

Jordan Blake: Quick check: landing pages must NOT send enterprise prospects to “Talk to sales” flow via retargeting. We said guardrail is activation-first + exclude active opps.

Tyler Benson: Yep landing page mapping below (activation-first). Also I’ll add exclusions via CRM list + domain suppression. Isabelle — can you confirm the exclusion list format is ready weekly?

Isabelle Laurent: Yes. Weekly export (active opps + “do not market” accounts) to the suppression list. I’ll send you the automation spec after this thread.

Ben Carter: landing pages: can we keep it to 2 pages max? attribution gets messy when we split too much.

Tyler Benson: Proposed mapping:
- Audience `site_visitor` -> `/docs/quickstart` (with “Hello world” snippet + sign-up CTA)
- Audience `signup_no_activation` -> `/console/api-keys` (deep link; if not logged in, redirects to login/signup then back)
- Audience `activated_no_return` -> `/console/usage` (show usage + “enable streaming” nudge + examples)
If we need a non-console option for `site_visitor` on mobile, fallback to `/docs/quickstart?ref=retgt`.

Tessa Morgan: Design note: if we’re sending to `/console/api-keys`, make sure the first screen has context for new users (not just empty state). Otherwise ads will look “broken”. We can add a small top banner behind a flag.

Ben Carter: +1. We have the nudge component already; can reuse with copy like “Create an API key to make your first request”.

Soojin Lee: Copy consistency: for `site_visitor` ads landing on docs quickstart, we should mirror phrasing: “First request in <5 minutes” + “predictable latency/cost”. Avoid “enterprise-grade” wording in these.

Tyler Benson: Great. Locking conventions:
- `utm_campaign=ri_retgt_q1_2026_activation`
- `utm_content={audience}-{creative}-{cta}` using the slugs above
- Landing pages per mapping above (console deep links for post-signup audiences)
I’ll update the retargeting sheet + HubSpot tracking + send final template links.

Camila Reyes: Approved. Tyler please post the final “UTM + LP mapping” in the launch brief thread too.

Tyler Benson: Will do. :thumbsup: (and I’ll add validation in the dashboard so any weird UTMs get flagged)

Ben Carter: :shipit:
